section.title: Board ID
section.paragraph: Board id can indicate PCB part number, serial number, memory type, etc. User can get board corresponding information they want from this feature. EC and BIOS can use this to distinguish board when programming.

list_item.paragraph: X86 - Main processors executing the x86 Instruction Set Architecture
PSP FW - Security firmware executed by the PSP
SBIOS - System Basic Input Output System
Factory flashed board id to EEPROM before leaving the factory.
EC will R/W the EEPROM to get or flash board id table.
BIOS can also get and flash board id through EC RAM.

section.title: Feature Implementation Details
section.paragraph: The original data read from I2C EEPROM is stored in below struct. And it maps same as format in I2C EEPROM.

list_item.paragraph: Then EC will cover the original data to internal buffer, which is reported to host via ECRAM.
The PCB revision and board id are converted to logic number. While SKU, hardware level and memory type are same as original value.
Host can read below struct from ECRAM offset 0x93. (byte0 - 0x93, byte1 - 0x94 …..)

section.title: Firmware Interface
section.paragraph: EC to SBIOS interface for board id.

section.title: Feature Risk
section.paragraph: System can’t boot up or hang when EC or BIOS didn’t read correct board id. Some board flashed incorrect board id can’t boot up.

section.title: Feature Verification Unit Test Plan
section.paragraph: Before check board ID info, please get the correct info from Board design team. Board ID of a project is consistent. Boot to UEFI shell, run AMDBRDID_v0.4.efi, check Manufacture Area/User Area/Memory type/ECO Status, make sure all info can show correct.

section.title: Dependencies
section.paragraph: Board should have EEPROM to store board id. Make sure it has been flashed right board id.
